# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)

# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.

EndSection

### Post by Osamabingandhi on 2006-06-01
I have a geforce3 ti200 and possible it is the same problem. I cannot switch between my monitor and my lcd-tv....The only way i have discovered to do it is to unplug the one monitor that you don't want to use. My VGA is connected to my lcd and when it is plugged in i can only use my lcd-tv and no way to switch to my monitor with DVI...I'd like help with this too. What i have understood is that geforce3 isn't really twinview compatible, so to do that xorg.config editing is a bit problematic. I can't seem to get it to work anyways. I am a total noob so it isn't surprising...

---

### Post by Osamabingandhi on 2006-06-02
I've got a little solution, not a very good one but it works ok for me. I can swich monitors by disconnecting the vga-cable and logging out and logging in again with ctrl-atl-backspace. That is better than restarting the whole computer. I now have right resolutions for both my monitors. Here is my xorg.conf (sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf):

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA Corporation NV20 [GeForce3 Ti 200]"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	VideoRam	64000
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
        Option "Twinview" "TRUE"
        Option "NoLogo" "true"
        Option "Metamodes" "1280x768,1280x768; 1280x1024,1280x1024; 1280x768,NULL; 1280x1024,NULL"
        Option "SecondMonitorHorizSync" "30-60"
        Option "SecondMonitorVertRefresh" "50-60"      
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-60
	VertRefresh	50-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"NVIDIA Corporation NV20 [GeForce3 Ti 200]"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

---

I have tried to make my card use twinview but i am pretty sure it doesn't work. It is quite annoying to unplug my moniter everytime i want to change monitor....but that seems to be the only alternativ.
